statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

articles.headline: WNBA awards: A'ja Wilson a familiar MVP favorite, but keep an eye on Allisha Gray
articles.description: Andre Snellings takes a look at the where the awards races stand and identifies his favorite bets to make.

images.name: Allisha Gray [608x342]
images.caption: A'ja Wilson is the MVP favorite again, but Allisha Gray is worth tracking with her strong start.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: Olivia Miles is is off to a fast start for the Lynx and is the odds-on favorite for Rookie of the Year.

articles.headline: Fever reporter claims credential revoked over Clark reporting
articles.description: The Fever revoked the credentials of reporter Scott Agness, who said the team told him the decision was made because of "the spread of inaccurate and unsubstantiated information."

images.name: Indiana Fever logo [600x400]
images.credit: M. Anthony Nesmith/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

images.name: Caitlin Clark rattles a trey for Fever
images.caption: Caitlin Clark rattles a trey for Fever

articles.headline: Fever clear air in 'much-needed' team meeting, Cunningham says
articles.description: Fever guard Sophie Cunningham said players and coaches had a frank, "long" conversation intended to hash things out and get everyone back on the same page following back-to-back losses on the West Coast.

images.name: Caitlin Clark, Stephanie White shut down talk of rift
images.caption: Caitlin Clark, Stephanie White shut down talk of rift

articles.headline: A’ja Wilson's 25 points, 15 rebounds and 5 blocks lead the Aces past the Sparks 79-69
articles.description: — A’ja Wilson had 25 points, 15 rebounds and five blocks, Jackie Young added 16 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Los Angeles Sparks 79-69 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Kayla Thornton makes 5 of Valkyries' franchise-record 18 3-pointers in 95-77 win over Fire
articles.description: — Kayla Thornton made five 3-pointers and had 19 points and eight rebounds, Janelle Salaun came off the bench to contribute 18 points and the Golden State Valkyries jumped out to a big lead before holding off a late rally by the Portland Fire for a...

articles.headline: Kiki Iriafen has another double-double and the Mystics beat the Sky 90-72
articles.description: — Shakira Austin scored 17 points, Kiki Iriafen had 15 points and 11 rebounds, and the Washington Mystics beat the Chicago Sky 90-72 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Rhyne Howard scores 36 and Allisha Gray adds 26 in the Dream's 91-75 victory over the Sun
articles.description: — Rhyne Howard had 36 points with eight 3-pointers, Allisha Gray added 26 points, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Connecticut Sun 91-75 on Tuesday night for their fourth straight win in the series.

articles.headline: Fantasy women's hoops: Who's put together the steadiest campaigns so far in 2026?
articles.description: Eric Moody breaks down the fantasy mainstays so far this season, and others who could break into that tier.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1296x729]
images.caption: A'ja Wilson is still the gold standard for consistent fantasy production.
images.alt: Las Vegas Aces center A'ja Wilson (22) holds the ball during a break in the action against the Golden State Valkyries in the third quarter at Chase Center.

images.name: 260527 Nyara Sabally [1296x729]
images.caption: Nyara Sabally (right) of the Toronto Tempo scored a career-high 29 points on Thursday.

images.name: Emily Engstler [1296x729]
images.caption: Emily Engstler has 10 blocks and six steals in her past three games.

images.name: Jovana Nogic [1296x729]
images.caption: Jovana Nogic's 3-pointers have been very important to the Mercury's success, and she's available in over 50% of ESPN fantasy leagues.
images.alt: Phoenix Mercury guard Jovana Nogic smiles as she celebrates a 3-pointer against the Chicago Sky during the first half of a WNBA basketball game, Friday, May 15, 2026, in Phoenix.
